attempt.claim: Erdos #307: finite local obstructions (parity, mod-8, per-prime quadratic-residue, avoidance of primes 3 mod 4) provably cannot bound the primes in a squarefree arithmetic-derivative 2-cycle. Constructively (verified): for any target one builds an arbitrarily large prime set U with all r = 1 mod 8 plus a constructed prime t such that N/r (N=prod U) is a quadratic residue mod every r in U and A(U) = |U| mod 8 is free, with reciprocal mass > 2. Hence the decisive control point is the GLOBAL double-square lemma (A(U) +/- 2 M(U) both perfect squares), not its finite congruence shadows. This maps the barrier; it does not settle the problem.
attempt.detail: {"construction":"primes r = 1 mod 8 via Dirichlet; quadratic reciprocity makes prod eps_i = +1 so N/r is a QR mod every r; A(U) = |U| mod 8 controllable; reciprocal mass driven past the AM-GM threshold 2.","implication":"the Lean congruence filters in Erdos307.lean (parity, mod-8, QR, no-3-mod-4) are individually correct but CANNOT combine into a uniform bound; stop the congruence-filter search lane.","literature":"Ufnarovski-Åhlander 2003 Conjecture 4 (period-2 prime reciprocal); their Conj 3 (n^(k)=n) one-way implies it. Fixed-antiderivative finiteness (n'=a has n<=a^2/4) insufficient for 2-cycles (both values vary).","not_proved":["nonexistence of a 2-cycle","the double-square sufficient lemma"],"provenance_note":"GPT-Pro produced the barrier construction + sufficient lemma; Opus verified the QR construction numerically and the citations via Crossref.","result_type":"barrier / no-go for the congruence approach","sufficient_lemma":"No squarefree N with sum_{r|N} 1/r >= 2 has both D(N)-2N and D(N)+2N perfect squares. (Would immediately prove #307 nonexistence, since a 2-cycle gives D(N)-+2N = (M(P)-+M(Q))^2.)","verifier_script":"scripts/verify_307_barrier.py"}
